title: CVV Brute-Force With Strategic Success Positioning
narrative: Fraudster systematically tests stolen card numbers by cycling through CVV combinations (typically 000-999) until achieving authorization, then immediately executing high-value transactions. Pattern shows 8-15 declined transactions with decline_code 'N' (CVV mismatch) clustered within 45-90 seconds, all under $1.00 on digital ProductCD='W', followed by 1-3 successful transactions of $200-$850 within 2-5 minutes. The successful attempts often shift to ProductCD='C' or 'H' (physical goods). Fraudster uses same card1, addr1, and P_emaildomain across all attempts but may rotate dist1 values. TransactionAmt pattern shows exact values like $0.50 or $0.99 during testing phase, then jumps to amounts ending in .00 or .99 during exploitation phase.
indicators: 8-15 consecutive declined transactions with decline_code='N' within 45-90 seconds
card1_txn_count_24h increases from 0 to 15+ within single session
TransactionAmt under $1.00 during failures, then $200+ within 5 minutes of first success
ProductCD switches from 'W' (digital) during testing to 'C'/'H' (physical) post-success
Identical P_emaildomain and addr1 across all attempts with same card1
card1_amt_zscore spikes from near-zero to 3.5+ after successful CVV match
Time gap between last decline and first success: 8-45 seconds
summary: A burst of 8-15 sub-dollar transactions decline with CVV mismatch codes within 90 seconds, then 1-3 high-value purchases ($200-$850) succeed minutes later using the same card and email but shifting from digital to physical goods.
reasoning: The attacker is systematically guessing CVV codes on a stolen card number until finding the correct one, then immediately cashing out with expensive purchases before the cardholder notices or the card is blocked.
indicator_explanations: Rapid-fire string of failed transactions shows systematic CVV guessing attempts
Card goes from dormant to hyperactive in minutes during testing spree
Tiny test amounts suddenly jump to high-value once CVV is cracked
Purchase type shifts from throwaway digital to valuable physical goods after breakthrough
All attempts use identical email and address showing automated script control
Spending pattern explodes from baseline after attacker finds working CVV code
Success follows final failure suspiciously fast indicating immediate exploitation window
